title: Simagic Alpha Evo launches (9–28 Nm)
note: New 5-pole low-inertia servos + the third-party-friendly QR-A quick release reset the mid-tier value bar.

title: Fanatec ClubSport DD price cut (~$999 → ~$700)
note: Post-Corsair price reductions across the Fanatec DD line.

title: ClubSport DD firmware lifts torque 12 → 15 Nm
note: Free firmware update raised peak torque for existing owners.

title: Fanatec adds 3-yr warranty + 30-day returns
note: Corsair-era stabilization plus a unified Fanatec app — confidence restored.

title: MOZA R3 — first console DD bundle (~$279)
note: A complete 3.9 Nm direct-drive bundle for Xbox/PC, detonating the entry tier.

title: MOZA AY210 force-feedback yoke (~$699 base)
note: Real control loading at near-HOTAS money — FFB goes mainstream-adjacent for flight.

title: WinWing rebrands to WinCTRL
note: Plus the CyberTaurus FFB platform and announced FFB rudder pedals shipping 2026.

title: Honeycomb Charlie rudder pedals ship
note: Aluminum, belt-drive, Hall-effect — completing the all-Honeycomb GA flight deck.

title: Star Citizen adds experimental VR (Alpha 4.5)
note: A decade-old promise lands; an official settings toggle followed in 4.6 — reigniting head-tracking vs VR debate.

title: Elite Dangerous: Operations (zero-G + megaship combat)
note: New flight-and-FPS content renews cockpit + controller buying interest.

title: WinWing Ursa Minor reframes budget HOSAS
note: Magnetic sensors + in-stick vibration at ~$115/stick undercut the boutique tier.

title: SkyTrak ST MAX (faster processor, shorter delay)
Uneekor EYE MINI CORE (~$1,499, Amazon exclusive)
note: Genuine photometric infrared tech drops into the budget bracket.

title: Bushnell Launch Pro base ~$2,499 (Circle B Edition)
note: Lower entry price — but full sim still needs the Gold/GSPro subscription.

title: Rapsodo MLM2PRO gets official GSPro integration
note: Collapses the price of GSPro-ready hardware toward $700.

title: E6 Apex adds 7,000+ courses; Trackman Virtual Golf 3 (LIDAR)
note: The visual bar for 'realistic' rises across the software ecosystem.

title: Professional Ship Simulator (ex-NauticXP) heads to Early Access
note: 9 axes, 100+ buttons, HOTAS rudder support — the most hardware-friendly marine sim ever announced.

title: VR sailing matures on Quest 3 (MarineVerse + NauticEd)
note: Hand-tracked tiller and sheet sidestep the helm-hardware problem entirely.
